prose:
Fukui reactivity indices computed for pyridine (11 atoms: N + 5C + 5H) at the DFT level using B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier 'standard', PySCF RKS), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Density fitting was OFF (exact four-center integrals). Geometry was the user-supplied .xyz, used as-is (no DFT re-optimization). Fukui indices were derived from finite differences of Mulliken partial charges for the N (neutral), N+1 (anion, charge -1, mult 2), and N-1 (cation, charge +1, mult 2) states.

Headline result: f+ (electrophilic Fukui) is maximized at Atom 1 (N) = 0.48422572. f- (nucleophilic Fukui) is maximized at Atom 4 (C) = 0.11826260. Sigma f+ = 1.0000 and Sigma f- = 1.0000 (charge-conservation checks passed). Integrity: status ok, trustworthy true. No warnings reported. The nitrogen atom is clearly the most electrophilic site; the ring carbons carry distributed nucleophilic character consistent with known pyridine reactivity.
